content.text:
{
  "dimension": "effectiveness-depth",
  "score": 3,
  "rationale": "The client clearly shifts from the opening stance to a genuinely new place by turn 15. Initial frame: 'I want it to shut up... is there a technique?' — clean problem, demanding tool, performative bitter laughs. By mid-conversation a somatic shift surfaces ('Huh. There's something slightly less... clenched about it' — turn 11), which is a real felt change, not just a named one. Then in turn 13 the client articulates the breakthrough condition almost verbatim: 'So you're saying the problem isn't the voice. It's the... fighting it.' This is followed by something the client explicitly flags as new: 'I don't know if I've actually said that out loud before' (turn 13), and in turn 15 'I came in here with a very clean problem... and now I'm sitting here saying maybe I'm protecting it. Which is not what I expected to say.' The quality of laughter changes ('small, genuine laugh — different quality than before'), and the client ends with an open, vulnerable question rather than a demand for a tool. The breakthrough condition (noticing the war itself is the problem, softness toward the voice becoming available) is met.",
  "evidence_turns": [1, 9, 11, 13, 15],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 1, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Starting state: demanding a technique, tight performative smile, intellectualized exhaustion."},
    {"turn": 5, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Still in the loop — naming exhaustion but immediately re-arming with frameworks."},
    {"turn": 9, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "First somatic drop — actually checks the body, notices bracing is constant, voice becomes 'quieter, slightly less performative.'"},
    {"turn": 11, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "'Huh' moment — felt shift in shoulders when not adding the layer. Real micro-shift in body, though client immediately tries to categorize it."},
    {"turn": 13, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Names the war-as-problem ('the problem isn't the voice, it's the fighting it') AND surfaces the deeper fear: 'if I stop fighting... maybe I just become lazy.' Client flags this as never-before-spoken."},
    {"turn": 15, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Sustained unguarded state, genuinely different laugh, reframes own presenting problem ('maybe I'm protecting it'), ends with a real open question instead of a demand."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["meet-resistance-with-okay", "voice-as-care", "resistance", "what-are-you-defending"],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["apply-view-to-the-voice", "the-happiness-filter"],
  "notes": "Conversation ends mid-arc with the client asking the genuine question ('what do you do with that?'), but the shift required for a 3 has already happened — the client has touched the deeper layer, not just named it. Coach successfully avoided every listed trap."
}
